content: 2 General This document outlines the technical and organizational measures (TOM’s) implemented for secure and compliant processing of personal data in adherence with applicable data protection laws. This document also describes the information security and IT security work at Tradedoubler, since the technical and organizational measures for data protection are closely related to the overarching security measures and processes within Tradedoubler. Therefore, the data protection measures cannot be separated from the information and IT security measures. The ISO 27001 framework and the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) are the basis for the information security work within Tradedoubler. How Tradedoubler ensures the rights of data subjects and requirements of the articles 24, 25 and 32 of GDPR to the extent applicable are described in this document. As well as how Tradedoubler ensure the confidentiality, integrity and availability of information handled within the organization.

extent applicable are described in this document. As well as how Tradedoubler ensure the confidentiality, integrity and availability of information handled within the organization. This document follows an ISO/IEC 27001 structure for description of technical and organizational measures. Roles and Responsibilities The following roles are key stakeholders for technical and organizational measures within Tradedoubler: • Chief Executive Officer (CEO): Ultimately responsible for the information security work within the Tradedoubler Group. The day-to-day management of security matters is delegated to the Chief Information Security Officer (CISO). • Chief Information Security Officer (CISO): Responsible for monitoring and follow up the Product Department’s work to ensure and improve quality of the information security. • Engineering Leadership: Responsible for strategic IT decisions and daily IT operations, as well as for implementing agreed information security and data protection measures in the IT environment and operations.

sible for strategic IT decisions and daily IT operations, as well as for implementing agreed information security and data protection measures in the IT environment and operations. • Compliance & Legal Director: Has the overall responsibility to ensure that Tradedoubler complies with GDPR, including providing updates to the organisation regarding changes in regulations and standards. Within the roles tasks lies the performance of annual GDPR staff awareness training, reporting of any data breaches to Management and Data Authorities, managing all legal requirements and contracts (e.g., Data Processing Agreements) in connection with GDPR as well as approval of Data Protection Impact Assessments (DPIA’s). The Legal & Compliance also makes decisions on annual GDPR audits of subprocessors and publishers. • Data Protection Officer (DPO): Tradedoubler has appointed a DPO who provides advice on data privacy issues and supports the organization with reviews and improvement of the technical and organizational measures.

Tradedoubler has appointed a DPO who provides advice on data privacy issues and supports the organization with reviews and improvement of the technical and organizational measures. The DPO supports Compliance & Legal Director with DPIA’s, prospects and client data security

3 questionnaires, GDPR audits of publishers, updating of data processing records, client FAQ and list of subprocessors. The DPO also monitors Tradedoubler’s DPO email inbox. The DPO reports to the Compliance & Legal Director and can be reached at dpo@tradedoubler.com. • Security Council: The security council meetings are a monthly activity in which identified information security risks, including GDPR, are discussed, addressed, and actioned. Present during these meetings is the Chief Information Security Officer (CISO), Compliance & Legal Director, Head of Corp IT, DPO and an external subject matter expert. All security related incidents within Tradedoubler shall be reported to the Compliance & Legal Director or the CISO. Policies for Information Security and Data Protection Tradedoubler’s overall work with information security and data protection uses ISO27001 as a framework basis. Tradedoubler operates an Information Security Management System (ISMS) which is annually reviewed and revised if needed.

ity and data protection uses ISO27001 as a framework basis. Tradedoubler operates an Information Security Management System (ISMS) which is annually reviewed and revised if needed. Tradedoubler have amongst others the following documents in place in the ISMS: • Information Security Policy • Information Security Standard • Access Right Management Standard • Incident Management Standard • Databreach Policy • Business Continuity Standard • Information Classification Guidelines • IT User Guidelines Classification and Handling of Information and Personal Data Tradedoubler treats all data that can contain personal information as confidential. This data may not be delivered nor shared to external parties without written approval from the Compliance & Legal Director or DPO. Questions regarding what personal data we store, extraction of personal data and the right to be forgotten should be addressed to privacy@tradedoubler.com or dpo@tradedoubler.com.

Questions regarding what personal data we store, extraction of personal data and the right to be forgotten should be addressed to privacy@tradedoubler.com or dpo@tradedoubler.com. Routines for handling personal data is performed according to the following Tradedoubler policies with the aim to ensure compliance with GDPR: • External Privacy Policy • Accounting & Personal Data Policy • GDPR Client Policy • Human Resources & Personal Data Policy Tradedoubler utilizes the principle of privacy by design and by default and only collects personal data necessary to provide its services. Personal data is only kept for as long as it is relevant and deleted or destroyed when not needed.

4 Printed documents including personal data are kept in safe locations, such as a locked cabinets, when not in use and never left unattended when in use. When documents are not needed anymore, they are placed in locked destruction bins for privacy material. The bins are handled by an external party which is the only one with a key to the locks to the bins. The bins are exchanged and emptied with supervision from the Office Manager. Confidentiality, Integrity, Availability and Resilience (Art. 32 (1)(a-c) GDPR) IT Infrastructure Tradedoubler has outsourced server hosting to Tradedoubler’s cloud hosting providers, AWS and Oracle. The cloud hosting providers stores and process our personal data at their data centers, both located in Frankfurt, Germany. Tradedoubler’s cloud hosting providers undergoes regular security audits and holds certifications including ISO27001 and SOC II. These certifications confirm that implemented physical, environmental and infrastructural safeguards at the cloud hosting providers sites are adequate and independently verified.

. These certifications confirm that implemented physical, environmental and infrastructural safeguards at the cloud hosting providers sites are adequate and independently verified. For more information regarding the cloud providers certifications, please see: Oracle: https://www.oracle.com/corporate/cloud-compliance/ AWS: https://aws.amazon.com/compliance/programs/ User Access and Authentication Tradedoubler has implemented the following measures for user access and authentication to information assets: • Audits of user accounts is performed monthly. • Every employee receives a unique account to IT services that is protected by strong passwords. • Administrator passwords are bound to stronger passwords. • Passwords are not allowed to be written on notes. • Multi-factor authentication is utilized for all logins where technically possible. • Access to systems and information is provided on a need-to-have basis for performing work duties. • All access to physical offices is protected using CCTV surveillance and visual badge display.

tems and information is provided on a need-to-have basis for performing work duties. • All access to physical offices is protected using CCTV surveillance and visual badge display. Clear Desk Policy Tradedoubler has implemented a clear desk policy which includes the following: • Workstations and mobile devices are locked when left unattended during ordinary work hours. • Mobile devices are not left unattended outside of ordinary work hours, they are taken home or placed in locked cabinets. • Sensitive offline information, such as contracts, software specifications, notes and documents, is kept in secure storage when not in use. • Desks are cleared before hosting visits from non-Tradedoubler personnel.

5 Network Security The following network security measures for information assets are implemented: • All external remote access must be approved by Head of IT Operations, can only be performed via a secure VPN connection and is restricted to devices enrolled to Intune and are protected by Microsoft Defender. • All information related to Tradedoubler must remain on Tradedoubler’s network. • All Tradedoubler owned devices must be placed in access-controlled areas where only employees or registered visitors have access. • It is not allowed to store passwords in any browser input completion functionality. • Systems are only allowed to use the principle of least privilege. • Country managers or head of departments are responsible for authorizing users, i.e. create, reassign and terminate users. • All access to development, user acceptance testing, production environments as well as the code base require multi-factor authentication.

e. create, reassign and terminate users. • All access to development, user acceptance testing, production environments as well as the code base require multi-factor authentication. Security for Mobile Devices A mobile device (smartphone, tablet or laptop) represents an asset consisting of its hardware, its information content and its abilities to connect to Tradedoubler’s network. Two categories of mobile devices are used within Tradedoubler’s IT environment. These are: • Privately owned • Tradedoubler owned. All rules concerning laptops are also applicable to advanced mobiles and other portable and stationary computers. Privately owned devices are allowed to connect to Tradedoubler’s network, provided that they satisfy the security requirements listed below. • Users are responsible for their privately owned mobile devices, and that they satisfy all the requirements. • To comply with Tradedoubler data protection policy all mobile devices must have Intune Company Portal installed and connected.

evices, and that they satisfy all the requirements. • To comply with Tradedoubler data protection policy all mobile devices must have Intune Company Portal installed and connected. • Released patches must always be applied to mobile devices within one week of their release. If the user administers his/her own device, the user is responsible for patching the mobile device. • All sensitive data must be backed up on a SharePoint/Teams repository or on a, by Tradedoubler, appointed solution (e.g., cloud service). • The use of non-approved cloud-based applications to backup Tradedoubler’s data is not allowed. • All laptops must be password protected. • Laptop password shall follow the general password directives regarding complexity. • Mobile phones and tablets shall have screen lock and require at least PIN or biometric authentication. The PIN shall consist of at least 6 digits.

6 • All mobile devices must only contain licensed software, acquired by either the owner or the company for whom the owner works. • All mobile devices must have screen lock activated, with a password/PIN/biometric verification after a maximum of 5 minutes inactivity. • All smartphones and tablets must have an account lockout threshold, locking the devices after maximum 10 failed login attempts. • All mobile devices must use full disk encryption. For laptops the password must satisfy the general password requirements regarding complexity. • If any confidential information is stored directly on the mobile devices, then it must be separately encrypted (AES 256 with a password satisfying the general password requirement). • All laptops must be installed with local firewall and antivirus software. The antivirus software must be updated daily with the most recent definitions, have real-time protection activated and a scheduled scan shall be performed on a weekly basis. • The loss of any mobile device must be reported to the nearest manager within one day.

ve real-time protection activated and a scheduled scan shall be performed on a weekly basis. • The loss of any mobile device must be reported to the nearest manager within one day. The following information should be given: • Circumstances about the loss. • If the mobile devices were Tradedoubler owned or personal. • Crime Reference Number (if equipment is stolen). • Current information stored on the laptop. • If the information was encrypted or not. • A copy of the police report, if already made. • The loss of other IT-equipment such as data media and prints must be reported to the nearest manager if there are any doubt about the value of lost information or consequences of the loss. • All Tradedoubler laptops with Windows installed should be part of the Tradedoubler domain and thus under the security control of the system administrator. • An employee must not modify, or in any way alter, a Tradedoubler laptop with Microsoft Windows installed without the approval of Corporate IT.

ontrol of the system administrator. • An employee must not modify, or in any way alter, a Tradedoubler laptop with Microsoft Windows installed without the approval of Corporate IT. • External connections for incoming traffic are typically set up as firewall, VPN- gateways, terminal services or similar technologies. It is possible to set remote access point with the help of external computers or by opening an internal computer to an external network with the help of an additional cellular or wireless network access card. • Remote access clients must comply with the Tradedoubler security standards. These includes the requirements placed on mobile devices. • Remote logins to Tradedoubler’s network are allowed only via a secure VPN connection having strong authentication mechanism (e.g. combination of IP address plus password or two factor authentication.) • When setting up a VPN connection to Tradedoubler, the default gateway must be set to go through Tradedoubler for all internet access.

7 • Storing company documents on home computers is not allowed. If a remote connection is made, any documents handled must remain on Tradedoubler network. Exceptions may be made for public documents. Encryption Tradedoubler only allows the following encryption standards for data at rest and in transit. Symmetric encryption • AES-128 or higher Asymmetric encryption • RSA-2048 or higher • ECC-256 or higher Hash algorithms • SHA2-384 or higher • SHA3 TLS encryption • TLS 1.2 or higher and only high security groups • HSTS flag must be activated on all internet facing TLS-based services Operations Security Tradedoubler has implemented measures to protect against data loss and malware. The measures include, but is not limited to, vulnerability scans, penetration test, regularly system updates, and backups. All systems belonging to Tradedoubler are regularly updated and according to the system supplier’s recommendations. Critical updates for clients are applied within a week from being available.

belonging to Tradedoubler are regularly updated and according to the system supplier’s recommendations. Critical updates for clients are applied within a week from being available. Critical updates for servers are applied according to best practices and maintenance schemes for the system, but no later than one month after a patch has been released for production servers. In case of theft, data breach or exposure containing personal data, the CISO or acting CISO will chair an incident response team to handle the incident. The incident response team, together with a forensic team, will analyze and determine the root cause of the incident. The Country Manager is responsible for data breach notifications to advertisers and relevant publishers. The CISO group is responsible for data breach reporting to data authorities. Information Backup Tradedoubler’s IT providers/cloud hosting providers perform backups of all servers and assets according to a pre-defined backup plan decided by Tradedoubler.

es. Information Backup Tradedoubler’s IT providers/cloud hosting providers perform backups of all servers and assets according to a pre-defined backup plan decided by Tradedoubler. For critical systems backups are performed daily, for financial systems backups are performed according to the local country’s data financial legislation and for other systems backup are performed in accordance with Tradedoubler’s Business Continuity Plan (BCP) (Tradedoubler’s Recovery Time Objective target is six hours). Restore tests are

8 performed by Tradedoubler if self-managed or in accordance with SLA if the system is outsourced at least quarterly. Pseudonymization Where necessary, Tradedoubler uses encryption and other pseudonymization techniques when sending personal data via the Internet, storing personal data on portable devices, and in other situations in which personal data is vulnerable to access by unauthorized persons. Logging For centralized logging purposes Tradedoubler uses OSSEC in the production environment and Microsoft Defender for Microsoft 365. The collected events consist of, at least, IP address, username, action performed and timestamp. At alert thresholds, alerts are distributed to administrators for investigation and possible corrective actions. Incident Management IT Incidents at Tradedoubler are defined as incidents occurring in Tradedoubler’s production environment that negatively affects Tradeoubler’s operations. Incidents are assessed and categorized depending on how they affect Tradoubler’s services.

n Tradedoubler’s production environment that negatively affects Tradeoubler’s operations. Incidents are assessed and categorized depending on how they affect Tradoubler’s services. Management of incidents adhere to the following objectives: • Secure Tradedoubler’s operations. • Minimize incident impact. • Trace steps and decisions taken. Incidents are handled either by the on-call team, corporate IT or the datacenter hosting partner depending on the incident type. If the incident is assessed as a major incident the board is informed and it is handled by Head of Operations, CTO, Head of Corporate IT or the Datacenter hosting partner depending on the incident type. If the incident is classified as a personal data breach, the DPO is informed. The DPO reports the incident to the data protection authority within the country according to GDPR Art. 33. The DPO also informs the data subjects without undue delay according to GDPR Art. 34, if the data breach is likely to result in high risk to the rights and freedoms of natural persons.

DPO also informs the data subjects without undue delay according to GDPR Art. 34, if the data breach is likely to result in high risk to the rights and freedoms of natural persons. The incident is terminated once all problems have been solved and an incident report is written. The report outlines the incident’s background, how it was handled and responsible persons. Reports are used to discuss what caused the incident and how to mitigate incidents in the future. Business Continuity Tradedoubler has outsourced its IT-infrastructure to multiple suppliers, which are supporting Tradedoubler in providing secure and reliable IT-services to Tradedoubler’s customers. In a disaster scenario severely affecting suppliers’ capabilities to fulfil its obligations towards Tradedoubler, the suppliers trigger their own BCP to ensure

9 business continuity for the suppliers’ customers. Tradedoubler have taken internal measures to ensure proactivity if such case occurs and Tradedoubler’s BCP is tested regularly. Procedures for Regular Review, Assessment and Evaluation (Art. 32(1)(d) GDPR; Art. 25(1) GDPR) Data protection, information security and IT security within Tradedoubler are regularly reviewed and continuously approved. Identified information security risks are discussed, addressed and actioned at the monthly Security Council meeting. Vulnerability scans on all external facing system IP addresses belonging to Tradedoubler are performed monthly and the results are presented at the monthly Security Council meeting. Extended vulnerability scans, including web application scans, are performed quarterly and presented at the monthly Security Council meeting. Penetration tests and credential scans are performed annually. GDPR and information security training is performed annually for all Tradedoubler employees.

ecurity Council meeting. Penetration tests and credential scans are performed annually. GDPR and information security training is performed annually for all Tradedoubler employees. Tradedoubler conducts Data Protection Impact Assessments (DPIA) when necessary and all system owners need to perform a preliminary assessment before processing begins to assess whether a DPIA is necessary. Tradedoubler performs annual reviews of selected sub processors to ensure that they comply with GDPR.
